svg.aria-label: Hammurabi commander system architecture — identity, memory, quest, automation, agent-side reads
svg.text: How identity, memory, quests, automations, and agent-side knowledge are loaded, written, and triggered
§1 BOOT — system prompt assembly (loaded ONCE at session start)
live snapshot of pending+active quests rendered into prompt · prompt.ts:101-120
embedded CLI cheat-sheet (claim / note / done) · see §5
last 6 scratch entries; no truncation logic above that
final = [Layer 0 + Layer 1 + Layer 2 + Layer 3] joined with double-newline
OUTPUT · passed once to provider runtime
System prompt is set once. MEMORY.md / LONG_TERM_MEM.md / working-memory NOT
re-loaded each turn. Heartbeat re-injection is via user-message body only — see §3.
§2 LIVE TURN — provider I/O during chat (Hammurabi only mirrors transcripts)
WebSocket broadcast → UI listeners (Hervald + Agents Monitor)
Outbound action / tool call → ProviderApprovalAdapter (claude | codex | gemini) → ActionPolicyGate
unified approval architecture (#1186) · policy auto-allows safe internal actions · outbound queues at `/api/approvals/pending` for human review
workers run in single approval-on `default` mode · no provider bypass · see also docs/diagrams/approval-routing-overview.svg
§3 HEARTBEAT — every 15 min, fat every 4th tick (the only Hammurabi-side mid-turn injection)
render stored heartbeatMessageTemplate → enqueue as user message · no re-loads
FAT tick (1 of 4) · contextMode = fat · buildFatHeartbeatMessage
⑤ assemble → enqueue as queued internal user message → consumed on next turn
sendQueuedInternalUserMessage() · routes/context.ts:1050-1053 · delivered as user-role, NOT system-prompt re-injection
⚠ Fat heartbeats do NOT re-inject MemoryContext (Layer 2/3). README §32: "Fat heartbeats no longer inject assembled memory layers."
§4 MEMORY WRITES — three paths, three writers, ONE invariant: agent reads only at session start
de-duped append-only · per-commander mutex · bumps remote-sync revision
⚠ NEVER overwrite. Bullet-list grows over time; cleanup is external (§6).
via Edit/Write tool against the file
distilled per-day journal narrative; rolled forward by external cron skill
⚠ no `hammurabi memory compact` CLI exists; cron reads .jsonl and writes file directly
JSON store + rendered .md mirror; both files written atomically
⚠ scratch only — do not promote durable conclusions here
⚠ NOT auto-RAG into prompt — search only when explicitly invoked
§5 QUEST BOARD — work queue surfaced into the prompt at boot, refreshed at each fat heartbeat
create · list · claimNext · update · note · del
Read paths (how the agent finds out what to do next)
① At session start → Layer 1 of system prompt (QuestBoardSection in §1)
② At each fat heartbeat → injected into user message body (step ② in §3)
③ Mid-turn, on demand → agent runs `hammurabi quests list` itself
⚠ No mid-turn system-reminder injection on quest mutation
A `quests claim`/`note`/`done` from another process or commander does NOT push a notification into the running session.
The commander discovers the new state via path ② or ③. Live state propagation is heartbeat-bounded (≤15 min).
§6 AUTOMATIONS — cron tasks + sentinels (run as their OWN sessions, do NOT inject into commander chat)
Cron tasks · CommandRoomScheduler (node-cron, registered on initialize)
STORAGE (3 tiers, all read by task-store.ts):
spawn fresh stream session named command-room-<taskId>-<ts>
spawn stream session · sessionType: 'sentinel' · parentCommanderId set
prompt seeded by seed-memory.ts · reads its own memory.md, doc.md
cron = stateless one-shot · sentinel = stateful long-lived monitor with own memory
⚠ Neither lands in the parent commander's chat. Communication is via shared files (MEMORY.md, quest board).
§7 AGENT-SIDE READS — Hammurabi runtime never loads these; the agent reads them itself via Read tool
behavioral instructions in CLAUDE.md / COMMANDER.md — fulfilled by agent at runtime
LOADED BY: Claude Agent SDK itself (NOT Hammurabi)
SDK walks directory tree from project root → cwd, auto-injects each CLAUDE.md as project file
Codex equivalent: AGENTS.md (same hierarchical walk, 32 KiB cap)
Path-scoped rules under .claude/rules/<file>.md (overall, agent-tooling, hammurabi, hervald, …)
Hammurabi server has zero direct reads of these files
⚠ Mutating these files mid-session does NOT reach an already-running agent
LOADED BY: agent itself, on demand, via Read tool
Behavioral instruction in ~/CLAUDE.md "Every Session" section directs agent to read these:
COMMANDER_GUIDE.md CLI / worker / memory / quest-board procedures
Shared across ALL commanders. Edit promotes one fact across the fleet.
LOADED BY: agent itself, on demand, via Read tool
Routing tables — "where does file X live?" / "which skill should I invoke?"
WORKSPACE.md /home/builder routing → which dir owns what
SKILLS_INDEX.md available skills + when to use each
Discovered new dir? Update WORKSPACE.md so future agents skip blind global search.
Zero matches in apps/hammurabi/ — confirmed agent-side only
★ marker = file is loaded into commander system prompt at session start (§1) · ⚠ marker = invariant or anti-pattern to keep in mind · dashed border = read-later artifact
Color sections: §1 boot (blue) · §2 live turn (green) · §3 heartbeat (amber) · §4 memory writes (rose) · §5 quest board (peach) · §6 automations (lavender) · §7 agent-side reads (grey)
